last_excerpt: it’s rehearsal, the mind drumming a question it isn’t ready to ask aloud the faucet doesn’t quite seal, tap…tap…tap, interval measured in seconds, an audible countdown to something ice cube shrinking in a glass, a soft click against the side in a crowd, sometimes an umbrella tip finds the sidewalk, a tap that’s almost an announcement, almost a warning the tap of a boot heel in a corridor, saying “someone is coming,” saying “wait” a typewriter, a teenager’s thumbs against a phone screen, the way language gets built out of tiny collisions a credit card against the reader, “just tap,” and the transaction completes tap, tap, tap, practicing a response in your head before the door opens sometimes the tap is just to check if something’s real—tapping the wall, tapping the pane, tapping a tooth tap tap tap sign that you’re still waiting sign that you’re still here sign that
last_concepts: the hum of a parking garage
the particular bounce of a flat tire
the way a hallway smells different at each end
the specific rattle of a spray paint can
pulling a sweater over your head
the way a street looks different when it's wet
a missing poster that's been up too long
a price tag left on a gift
the sound of a zipper on a tent
